package.name: us_army_operations_process
package.displayName: US Army Operations Process Foundry Ontology Accelerator
package.description: Doctrine-derived, unclassified Foundry ontology starter that models the US Army operations process (plan, prepare, execute, assess) and provides supporting object/action/link types for organizations, equipment, sustainment, and Joint Targeting Cycle (JTC) Phases 1-3. Intended to accelerate a Foundry build, not to be an authoritative or official Foundry export.
package.authoringNotes: All apiNames are stable Foundry-friendly identifiers. Property types use a small portable type vocabulary (string, integer, double, boolean, date, timestamp, geoshape, geopoint, attachment, marking, enum:<value_type_apiName>). Adapt to the destination tenant's exact Foundry property type names during import.

files.purpose: Enumerations and shared value types referenced by object, link, and action definitions.
Foundry-style object type definitions across operations process, JTC, organizations, equipment, sustainment, and geography.
Foundry-style link type definitions binding object types into a coherent operational graph.
Foundry-style action type definitions for operations process, JTC, sustainment, and equipment events.
All ten Army Classes of Supply (I-X) with descriptions, examples, and planning notes.
Joint Targeting Cycle Phase 1 (Commander's Objectives, Targeting Guidance, Intent), Phase 2 (Target Development and Prioritization), and Phase 3 (Capabilities Analysis).
The four Army operations process activities (Planning, Preparing, Executing, Assessing) with artifacts and actions.
Bibliography of doctrinal sources cited by sourceDoctrine references throughout the package.

validation.description: Validates JSON well-formedness and (when jsonschema is installed) conformance to the included JSON Schemas.
